message:
feat(search/tantivy + benches): governor-aware Tantivy writer threads
+ criterion benches for the perf refactors introduced with the
machine-responsiveness governor

Two pieces of plumbing that make the responsiveness governor work
landed in 3f947725 measurable *and* applied to one more hot fan-out
site.

### `src/search/tantivy.rs`

Add `tantivy_writer_parallelism_hint_for_available_governed(available_\
parallelism)` alongside the existing pure
`tantivy_writer_parallelism_hint_for_available`. The governed variant:

* Calls the formula-only variant to get the raw hint.
* Pipes that through `crate::indexer::responsiveness::effective_\
  worker_count(raw)` so Tantivy's writer thread count shrinks in lock-
  step with the governor when the host is under pressure (same
  behaviour on idle hosts; shrink + hysteresis regrow under load).
* Clamps to at least 1 so we never hand Tantivy zero writer threads.

Switch the public entry-point `tantivy_writer_parallelism_hint()` to
call the governed variant. The ungoverned `_for_available` form stays
`pub(crate)` so the pure formula-only unit tests elsewhere in the
module stay deterministic and don't have to pin the governor.

### `benches/index_perf.rs`

Add two criterion benches so the perf claims made in 3f947725 can be
re-run without hand-editing bench harnesses.

* `bench_semantic_embedding` sweeps
  `CASS_SEMANTIC_BATCH_SIZE ∈ {32, 128, 256}` × `CASS_SEMANTIC_PREP_\
  PARALLEL ∈ {1, 0}` over a 2 000-message corpus built by
  `build_semantic_corpus`. The corpus intentionally mixes:
    - plain prose lines
    - markdown (`**bold**`, `_italic_`)
    - fenced rust code blocks
    - short single-line messages
    - whitespace-heavy lines
    - combining-accent + emoji unicode
    - an "everything bagel" mixed line with inline code, links,
      braces, curly quotes, and a long tail the canonicalizer can
      truncate
  so the bench exercises every branch of `canonicalize_for_\
  embedding`. Uses the `hash` embedder so the bench is fast,
  deterministic, and does not depend on an ONNX model download.
  Sets `sample_size(20)` because the prep is fast and variance is
  low. Env vars are set via `unsafe { std::env::set_var }` inside the
  bench closure and scrubbed after `group.finish()` so downstream
  benches in the same binary are not polluted.

* `bench_ingest_with_responsiveness` runs `run_index` at
  `corpus_size = 200` twice — once with `CASS_RESPONSIVENESS_DISABLE=0`
  (governor on) and once with `=1` (governor pinned at 100%) — so
  a reviewer can see whether the governor is silently costing
  throughput on an idle box. Re-creates the db file and tantivy
  directory between iterations so we measure a cold ingest each
  time. Uses `fastembed` for the embedder but with `semantic=false`
  + `build_hnsw=false` so the bench stays indexer-focused and does
  not pay for vector index build.

* Wire both new benches into the `criterion_group!` macro and leave
  the existing `bench_index_full`, `bench_streaming_vs_batch`, and
  `bench_channel_overhead` in their prior order so `criterion-compare`
  can diff against historical baselines without reordering.

No changes to runtime behaviour: the governed hint collapses to the
ungoverned hint on idle hosts, and the benches are opt-in via
`cargo bench`.

files.patch:
@@ -84,8 +84,20 @@ pub(crate) fn tantivy_writer_parallelism_hint_for_available(available_parallelis
     available_parallelism.max(1).clamp(1, max_threads)
 }
 
+/// Governor-aware variant of `tantivy_writer_parallelism_hint_for_available`.
+/// Returns the same value on an idle host but scales down when the machine
+/// responsiveness governor has shrunk the global capacity. Call this from
+/// production code paths; the ungoverned `_for_available` variant is kept so
+/// formula-only unit tests stay deterministic.
+pub(crate) fn tantivy_writer_parallelism_hint_for_available_governed(
+    available_parallelism: usize,
+) -> usize {
+    let raw = tantivy_writer_parallelism_hint_for_available(available_parallelism);
+    crate::indexer::responsiveness::effective_worker_count(raw).max(1)
+}
+
 pub(crate) fn tantivy_writer_parallelism_hint() -> usize {
-    tantivy_writer_parallelism_hint_for_available(
+    tantivy_writer_parallelism_hint_for_available_governed(
         std::thread::available_parallelism()
             .map(std::num::NonZeroUsize::get)
             .unwrap_or(1),
